title: Docusign FAQs﻿ for AI
heading_path: Docusign FAQs﻿ for AI
text:
Read frequently asked questions about the practices and policies that apply to the use of AI in Docusign products and programs.

Trust, privacy, and transparency have always been at the core of Docusign, and our work with AI is no different. This FAQ shares additional details about the use of AI in Docusign products.

title: Docusign FAQs﻿ for AI
heading_path: Docusign FAQs﻿ for AI
Does Docusign use my data for AI model training and improvement?
text:
Docusign uses anonymized and aggregated data from consenting customers to improve our AI models to deliver greater value for our customers.

With the introduction of the AI data controls setting on May 30, 2024, Docusign only uses your data to improve its AI models if both of the following are true:

- Your organization has agreed to share its data for AI training through your Docusign service agreement.
- The AI data controls setting is turned on in your account settings.

title: Docusign FAQs﻿ for AI
heading_path: Docusign FAQs﻿ for AI
What is the "AI data controls" setting?
text:
The AI data controls setting provides additional control for account administrators to manage whether anonymized data is used for training purposes:

- For customers who provide contractual consent, account administrators can turn this setting ***off*** or ***on*** at any time. The toggle is turned ***on*** by default.
- For customers who have not provided contractual consent, the in-product toggle is disabled and set to ***off***. It cannot be changed by an account administrator.

See the [AI Data Controls Setting FAQs](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fzd1707173174972&topicId=dcs1708531444159.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the AI data controls setting, including how to turn the setting on or off, and frequently asked questions (FAQs).") for more information about this setting.

title: Docusign FAQs﻿ for AI
heading_path: Docusign FAQs﻿ for AI
In cases where Docusign uses my data for AI model training, how is my data protected and secured?
text:
Docusign trains AI models on data from customers only if a customer has given consent. The data is anonymized and aggregated before training occurs.

Docusign takes a multi-pronged privacy and security-centric approach to protect data privacy and confidentiality including: anonymization, aggregation, and encryption.

The Docusign anonymization process removes personal identifiers, such as:

- Name
- Phone numbers
- Email addresses
- Social security numbers (in the U.S.)
- National ID numbers

These measures are designed to prevent data from being used to re-identify or trace back to an individual or organization. For additional security, data is encrypted in transit and rest.

title: Docusign FAQs﻿ for AI
heading_path: Docusign FAQs﻿ for AI
Which AI models does Docusign use for AI features?
text: Docusign employs a hybrid-model approach that combines proprietary models with best-in-class third-party LLMs. Proprietary models are developed in-house and are customized open-source AI models. [Third-party models](https://www.docusign.com/trust/privacy/subprocessors-list) are provided by third-party organizations and accessed via API. Docusign uses models from providers like Microsoft Azure OpenAI Service and Google’s Vertex AI, including Gemini models.

title: Docusign FAQs﻿ for AI
heading_path: Docusign FAQs﻿ for AI
How is customer data protected when Docusign uses third-party AI models?
text:
Docusign ensures robust protections are in place to safeguard the data privacy and security of our customers and users. When working with providers of third-party models, there is:

- **No Training by Third-Party Model Providers:** Customer data is not shared with third-party model providers to train their models for other customers. Anonymized data may be used to customize AI models for Docusign’s AI solutions. Customized models are only available for Docusign to use.
- **No Persistent Data Storage:** Third-party model providers do not store customer data persistently after the model processes the data and returns an output.
- **Responsible AI and Safety Filters:** Docusign uses safety, content, and bias filtering mechanisms to process AI inputs and outputs, ensuring responsible and ethical use.

title: Introduction to AI at Docusign
heading_path: Introduction to AI at Docusign
text:
Read about AI at Docusign, including the types of AI that Docusign uses, and how Docusign protects and uses your data.

Review the following topics to learn more about Docusign Iris.

title: Introduction to AI at Docusign
heading_path: Introduction to AI at Docusign
What is Docusign Iris ?
text:
Docusign Iris is the AI engine that powers AI features across the agreement lifecycle, enabling you to create agreements faster, commit with greater confidence, and manage for maximum business impact.

With Docusign Iris, customer can:

- **Boost revenue and cut costs with agreement insights:** Uncover key opportunities and take action with AI-powered dashboards.
- **Close agreements in record time:** Speed up agreement prep and negotiation with AI-powered solutions that help you enforce company standards and simplify approvals.
- **Minimize financial and legal risk:** Empower teams to decode legalese, avoid missteps, and stay sharp on contractual commitments with AI.

Important: In all cases, AI should be considered a tool to assist you with your work. Any AI findings or AI-produced content should always be reviewed for accuracy.

title: Introduction to AI at Docusign
heading_path: Introduction to AI at Docusign
What types of AI does Docusign use?
text:
Docusign uses both traditional AI and generative AI:

- **Traditional AI** focuses on analyzing existing data or content to deliver contextual information specific to that data.
- **Generative AI** can create new content, such as text, based on patterns learned from existing data.

By leveraging both types of AI, Docusign can deliver the best results for the task in our AI-assisted solutions.

To illustrate how this works, let’s look at Docusign Iris in AI Extension for CLM:

- A user uploads an agreement into CLM. Docusign Iris analyzes the agreement and provides useful insights about the agreement.
- AI is used to create a summary composed of key parts of the agreement, which allows users to quickly understand the content of an agreement and accelerate agreement review.
- AI is also used to extract important attributes from an agreement, such as effective date or document type. Understanding these attributes fuels key business and contract processes, improves contract discoverability, and generates better business intelligence from agreements.

By using both traditional AI and generative AI, Docusign Iris transforms static, unstructured agreement data into actionable insights.

Figure 1.  Docusign Iris Workflow

![A diagram of the Docusign Iris workflow.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fzd1707173174972/page/images/AI_worflow_diagram_240805.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDM5MjMsInNoZWFmIjoiZnpkMTcwNzE3MzE3NDk3MiJ9.zoOO5BskdQchtU0To_tZXvhufLPb0pXS1vf0GzfizAA&_LANG=enus "A diagram of the Docusign Iris workflow.")

title: Introduction to AI at Docusign
heading_path: Introduction to AI at Docusign
How do I know when AI is part of my product experience?
text: Docusign is working to ensure that there are consistent visual indicators across your product experience, similar to this ![A screenshot of the Bloom icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fzd1707173174972/page/images/AI_Bloom-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDM5MjMsInNoZWFmIjoiZnpkMTcwNzE3MzE3NDk3MiJ9.zoOO5BskdQchtU0To_tZXvhufLPb0pXS1vf0GzfizAA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Bloom icon.") icon, to specify where AI is being used. You may also see icons, product notifications, and notices indicating the product or feature is AI-assisted.

title: Introduction to AI at Docusign
heading_path: Introduction to AI at Docusign
How does Docusign protect and use my agreement data when I use AI-assisted experiences in Docusign?
text:
Docusign is committed to trust and transparency with AI. This includes safeguarding customer data when using Docusign Iris. Docusign's platform for AI follows robust data protection controls, including encryption and access controls.

Docusign does not use your data to improve its AI models unless both of the following are true:

- Your organization has agreed to share its data for AI training through your Docusign service agreement.
- The AI data controls setting is turned on in your account settings. For Docusign Insight, see the next FAQ.

To provide additional protection, Docusign aggregates and anonymizes data before it is used to train Docusign AI models.

Note: Data anonymization includes removing personal identifiers, such as:

- Names
- Phone numbers
- Email addresses
- Social security numbers (in the U.S.)
- National ID numbers

title: Introduction to AI at Docusign
heading_path: Introduction to AI at Docusign
Does the 'AI data controls' setting apply to data in Docusign Insight?
text:
No. This consent does not apply to data in Docusign Insight because it is a single tenancy platform. Docusign Insight customers can modify or create new models using their own data for their Insight environment, but the data is otherwise not used to improve Docusign AI models.

See the  [Docusign Insight landing page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=hac1706296665611&topicId=jny1706298930641.html) on the support site for more information about this product.

title: Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code (Beta)
heading_path: Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code (Beta)
text:
We’re currently running a beta for the Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code, an AI-powered assistant to help developers generate tokens, orchestrate APIs, configure brands, and resolve integration issues faster. Install the extension from the [Visual Studio Marketplace](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=DocuSign.docusign-copilot).

The Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code provides an AI-powered experience to help developers build integrations.

- Developers can ask questions to the extension. The extension includes a [question and answer](https://developers.docusign.com/tools/visual-studio-code-extension/#docusign-Q-and-A) functionality that responds to Docusign-specific questions.
- Developers can use the extension to [get an access token](https://developers.docusign.com/tools/visual-studio-code-extension/#get-an-access-token) that can be used for [authentication](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/).
- Developers can use this extension to [create or update brand](https://developers.docusign.com/tools/ai-assistant-vs-code/#update-brand-files-using-vs-code-extension) files in a Docusign account.

title: Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code (Beta)
heading_path: Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code (Beta)
Prerequisites
text:
Before you can use the Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code, you will need:

- A [GitHub Copilot](https://github.com/features/copilot) license or free trial.
- A Docusign developer account. You can [create one](https://www.docusign.com/developers/sandbox) for free at the [Developer Center](https://developers.docusign.com/).
- [Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/download). Be sure to install the [GitHub Copilot extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=GitHub.copilot) in VS Code.

title: Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code (Beta)
heading_path: Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code (Beta)
Get an access token
text:
The Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code can also help developers get started with authentication, automating some of the manual configuration that is usually required when setting up an [Integration key](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/configure-app/#integration-key) (IK). The Developer AI Assistant streamlines the [process of generating an access token](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/confidential-authcode-get-token/) and lets you generate a token with the simple `/getAccessToken` slash command.

![](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg height='840' width='804' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' version='1.1'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

![A screenshot of the Docusign Copilot extension generating an access token](https://images.ctfassets.net/aj9z008chlq0/4ZYiGgk4SLgMZqAFCITU33/a9048c7ef1e7d1cd49b36dfc9b96f03b/CopilotStep1.png?w=804&h=840&q=50&fm=png)

To invoke Docusign Developer AI Assistant, use Ctrl+Alt+I on Windows and Cmd+Shift+I on Mac. After logging in, you will be asked to answer some questions to help determine the [authentication type](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/choose/) that your integration will use. Then the Developer AI Assistant will help you to set up an [integration key](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/configure-app/#integration-key) (IK). Choose whether you want to generate an IK automatically, receive instructions on how to create an IK in the Docusign Admin UI, or use an existing IK.

![](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg height='1556' width='794' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' version='1.1'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

![A screenshot of the options displayed by Copilot when creating an integration key.](https://images.ctfassets.net/aj9z008chlq0/1bbCmg37NQ1yrvDYoPF8YL/176b547379ac097e77e8b99d5279f745/CopilotStep2.png?w=794&h=1556&q=50&fm=png)

If you choose to generate an IK through the Developer AI Assistant, you will be prompted to enter some information about the new IK. Once the IK has been created, you will see a confirmation like the one below in your chat window. In this example, an RSA key pair was generated for use with JWT authentication. If you choose Authorization Code Grant, the output will look slightly different.

![](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg height='830' width='810' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' version='1.1'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

![A screenshot of the RSA key pair generated by Copilot](https://images.ctfassets.net/aj9z008chlq0/3BSq8k2g6c5pIsoAh4i7Px/ed709f5a7107541a0a1cc47fd6fac6ba/GeneratedRSAKey.png?w=810&h=830&q=50&fm=png)

The Developer AI Assistant will continue to guide you through granting consent and generating an access token that will appear in the **OUTPUT** tab.

You can continue to use the Developer AI Assistant to learn how to use this access token to make calls to Docusign APIs including the [eSignature REST API](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/) and [more](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/).

![](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg height='906' width='1600' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' version='1.1'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

![A screenshot of the Docusign Visual Studio Code extension after an access token has been generated](https://images.ctfassets.net/aj9z008chlq0/4unFwiJJJabwCnbKVced1J/53d6a5de135f331d944e3af172c450c7/VisualStudioCopilotExtension.png?w=1600&h=906&q=50&fm=png)

title: Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code (Beta)
heading_path: Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code (Beta)
Update brand files using VS code extension
text:
Docusign administrators can manage their brand details either through the Docusign eSignature admin user interface or the Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code extension. Using the VS Code extension, administrators can create, download, and update brand files directly within the Visual Studio Code. This allows administrators to configure brand settings in Visual Studio Code and easily upload the brand files to their Docusign account. For more details on brand functionalities, see [Brands](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=lfr1583277366660.html).

Brand file updates using the VS Code extension are now available in demo environments only. This feature is in beta and not currently available in production.

Updating brand files directly in Visual Studio Code provides following benefits:

- Streamlines brand files management.
- Enables real-time editing, previewing, and verification.
- Removes manual file handling and uploading steps in the Docusign eSignature administrator interface.

Administrators can access these features using the Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code extension via the command palette (**Ctrl+Shift+P** on Windows/Linux or **Cmd+Shift+P** on macOS). With this extension, administrators can update brand XML files, including email templates, headers, footers, colors, fonts, logos, etc.

title: Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code (Beta)
heading_path: Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code (Beta)
Create a New Brand
text:
To create a new brand in your Docusign account using VS Code:

1. Open the Command Palette (Ctrl+Shift+P or Cmd+Shift+P) and run **Create Docusign Brand.**

   ![](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg height='152' width='603' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' version='1.1'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

   ![create docusign brand command](https://images.ctfassets.net/aj9z008chlq0/7qeglWI5xfKqG2YR5hqFXE/75fde72e84a9dff05b1715219e1e7ef0/create-docusign-brand.png?w=603&h=152&q=50&fm=png)
2. If prompted, sign in to your Docusign developer account and select your account.
3. Enter a name for the new brand and press Enter.
4. Choose a location to save the brand files.

   A success message will appear, and the brand files will open in a new VS Code window. You can also verify the new brand in your eSignature [Admin Brand settings](https://apps-d.docusign.com/admin/brands).

title: Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code (Beta)
heading_path: Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code (Beta)
Download a Brand
text:
To download files for an existing brand in your Docusign account using VS Code:

1. Open the Command Palette (Ctrl+Shift+P or Cmd+Shift+P) and run **Download Docusign Brand**.

   ![](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg height='161' width='598' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' version='1.1'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

   ![download-docusign-brand](https://images.ctfassets.net/aj9z008chlq0/kagMqjLuqKosEudFb793s/b17d274bb976be15f199460290186482/download-docusign-brand.png?w=598&h=161&q=50&fm=png)
2. If prompted, sign in to your Docusign developer account and select your account.
3. Select a brand from the list.
4. Choose a folder to save the brand files.

   The brand files will be downloaded and opened in a new VS Code window, including:

- **logo/** - contains brand logos
- **resources/ -** contains email templates and other assets. For more information, refer [Docusign Signing Resource File Guide](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=docusign-signing-resource-file-guide&topicId=docusign-signing-resource-file-guide.pdf&_LANG=enus).
- **brand.json** - contains brand profile information

title: Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code (Beta)
heading_path: Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code (Beta)
Editing Brand Files
text:
After you have downloaded the brand files into your preferred directory, the following file and folder structure will appear in your **VS Code** window:

| Path | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `/logo` | Contains the brand's logo image files. |
| `/resources` | Contains email templates and other assets used for brand customization. |
| `/resources/email/mockdata/values.yaml` | Lists the placeholder values used across all email templates. Modifying this file allows you to make global changes to your templates simultaneously during the development process.   **Note**: The values defined in this file are used **for local testing and development**. They are not uploaded when updating brand files to the server. To make the changes in the server, use [Admin Brand settings](https://apps-d.docusign.com/admin/brands) page. |
| `/resources/email/templates/` | Contains the actual email templates available for editing and previewing. |
| `brand.json` | Contains brand’s core metadata, including the brand ID, name, and supported languages. |

 **Real-Time Previewing**You can view your changes instantly without leaving your workspace. As you edit the files in `/resources/email/templates/`, you can open a **live preview** window directly within VS Code. This allows you to visualize layout adjustments, CSS tweaks, and content changes in real time, ensuring your design is perfect before updating the brand files.

![](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg height='680' width='1500' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' version='1.1'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

![brand files preview in vs code]()

**Dynamic Data & Conditional Logic**The values.yaml file contains the following values:

- **Placeholder Values:** Use keys from `values.yaml` to inject dynamic content (like brand names or links) throughout your templates. This ensures consistency and makes global updates at once.
- **Conditional Logic:** Uses conditional flags in values.yaml to toggle specific sections of an email based on your brand requirements. For example, the `IsEnvelopeContainingSingleDocument` flag controls grammatical plurality: setting it to **true** displays text in the singular form, while **commenting it out** defaults the text to the plural form.

**Note:** To disable a condition, you must **comment out** the line in the values.yaml file. Do **not** simply set the value to False, as the system will not recognize this as a disabled state.

![](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg height='479' width='1492' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' version='1.1'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

![vs code condition](https://images.ctfassets.net/aj9z008chlq0/2hYCmupR6HkTz9xw94qu9x/c1b3859dd46b0bc900bc606759f608b1/vscodecondition.png?w=1492&h=479&q=50&fm=png)

title: Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code (Beta)
heading_path: Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code (Beta)
Updating a Brand
text:
**Prerequisite:** Make sure your VS Code workspace contains the latest downloaded brand files from your Docusign account.

After editing the brand files, you can either:

- Update the brand files in your Docusign account, or
- Clone the brand files to create a new copy in your account

**Update the brand files folder**To update brand files in your Docusign account:

1. Open the Command Palette (Ctrl+Shift+P or Cmd+Shift+P) and run **Update Docusign Brand**.

   ![](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg height='150.00000000000003' width='603' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' version='1.1'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

   ![update-docusign-brand](https://images.ctfassets.net/aj9z008chlq0/1MicXiAWh1E5Aca6RSul4f/b9176259f47fe50ec6a1f7b194bbce11/update-docusign-brand.png?w=603&h=150&q=50&fm=png)
2. Select Update <brandname> Files.

   The extension will:
   - Combine local brand.json, resources, and logos
   - Push changes to your Docusign account
   - Update email templates (only **en-US** is supported) and other resources
   - Upload XML files and brand logos

If successful, a confirmation message appears. You can also verify the new brand in your eSignature [Admin Brand settings](https://apps-d.docusign.com/admin/brands). For validation errors, rectify the error and repeat the commands.

**Clone the brand files folder**To save modified brand files as a new brand:

1. Open the Command Palette (Ctrl+Shift+P or Cmd+Shift+P) and run **Update Docusign Brand**.

   ![](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg height='150.00000000000003' width='603' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' version='1.1'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

   ![update-docusign-brand](https://images.ctfassets.net/aj9z008chlq0/1MicXiAWh1E5Aca6RSul4f/b9176259f47fe50ec6a1f7b194bbce11/update-docusign-brand.png?w=603&h=150&q=50&fm=png)
2. Select Clone and Update <brandname> Files.

   The extension will:
   - Combine local brand.json, resources, and logos
   - Push changes to your Docusign account
   - Update email templates (only en-US is supported) and other resources
   - Upload XML files and brand logos
   - Create a new brand with the name suffixed by \_cloned and download the files to your machine
   A success message will appear, and the cloned brand files will open in a new VS Code window for editing.

title: Build with Docusign and AI
heading_path: Build with Docusign and AI
text: Docusign helps organizations prepare, sign, act on, and manage agreements. As large language models (LLMs) become a standard part of how developers work with APIs and documentation, Docusign is introducing tools that bring AI directly into the developer experience. From the [Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code (Beta)](https://developers.docusign.com/tools/ai-assistant-vs-code/), to markdown documentation that’s easy to copy into an LLM, or the [MCP Server](https://developers.docusign.com/tools/mcp-server/) that enables natural-language interactions with Docusign APIs, these tools are designed to reduce friction and speed up development. Together, they help developers get answers faster, explore capabilities more easily, and work with Docusign using AI in ways that fit naturally into existing workflows.

title: Build with Docusign and AI
heading_path: Build with Docusign and AI
The Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code
text:
The [Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code (Beta)](https://developers.docusign.com/tools/ai-assistant-vs-code/) is a Visual Studio Code extension that puts answers and examples right where you write code. By tagging @docusign in a Copilot Chat, you can ask about available APIs, walk through authentication flows, or get code snippets tailored to your project. The extension is designed to reduce the time you spend searching across documentation or switching contexts between your browser and editor.

Beyond convenience, the assistant ensures that the information you receive is accurate and aligned with official Docusign developer resources. It can also help you generate access tokens and test sample calls inside your local environment. This integration enables you to build and validate solutions without leaving VS Code, keeping your workflow focused and uninterrupted.

[Learn more about the Docusign Developer AI Assistant for VS Code →](https://developers.docusign.com/tools/ai-assistant-vs-code/)

MCP Server
text:
The Docusign MCP Server gives developers a way to work with Docusign APIs through natural-language interactions using any AI assistant that supports the MCP standard. It allows you to explore Docusign capabilities, test API calls, and more without switching between multiple tools or references. By interpreting plain-language prompts and translating them into the correct Docusign actions, the MCP Server makes integrations more intuitive and supports faster experimentation and automation.

[Learn more about the MCP Server →](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/mcp-server/)

title: Build with Docusign and AI

Markdown files and the role of LLM-optimized content
text:
Docusign documentation can now be viewed and copied in plain-text formats optimized for large language models.

On supported pages, you’ll see two new options that surface the underlying markdown content:

- **View as Markdown:** Opens the same page in a new browser tab with `.md` appended to the URL, displaying a clean, text-only version of the content.
- **Copy for LLM:** Instantly copies the page’s plain-text content so you can paste it into the LLM of your choice.

![](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg height='1000' width='1900' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' version='1.1'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

![LLM and Markdown button screenshot](https://images.ctfassets.net/aj9z008chlq0/4goPbNxPhgjXJhi63b9XHf/f95fce930bc939fc7b038a2f7e97c87c/LLM_Markdown.png?w=2088&h=1114&q=50&fm=png)

Markdown is a better input source for LLMs than scraping or copying from a rendered HTML page because:

- It avoids extra formatting tokens that can confuse an LLM.
- Content hidden in tabs or expandable sections is included in the markdown version, so nothing is lost.
- The structure and hierarchy of markdown headings are easy for models to parse.
- Clean, structured formatting improves accuracy and retrieval, helping LLMs return more precise answers.

The **Copy for LLM** button is simply a shortcut for grabbing the entire markdown output without opening the separate `.md` view. It gives you immediate access to the plain-text version of the page so you can paste it directly into an LLM with no extra steps.

When used together, the **View as Markdown** and **Copy for LLM** options give developers a simple way to bring Docusign documentation into AI-driven development. Whether you open the markdown view or copy the plain-text version directly into a model, both approaches ensure that your AI tool works with the same trusted material available in the Developer Center and formatted for clear, reliable consumption.

Currently, these options are available on [Use case](https://developers.docusign.com/use-cases/) pages, with broader support coming soon.
